news.title: Evaluation of the Networks for School Improvement Initiative—How Network Hubs Develop and Support Continuous Improvement Networks: Final Report
news.summary_en: In this report, the authors examine how intermediary organizations developed and supported 25 networks of schools using continuous improvement approaches to improve educational outcomes for students who are Black, Latino, or experiencing poverty.

news.title: Evaluation of the Networks for School Improvement Initiative—How Network Hubs Develop and Support Continuous Improvement Networks: Data Sources and Methodology
news.summary_en: The authors review the data and methods used to examine how intermediaries developed and supported 25 school networks using continuous improvement approaches to improve educational outcomes for students who are Black, Latino, or experiencing poverty.

news.title: Evaluation of Networks for School Improvement: Impacts, Implementation, and Lessons for Network-Based Continuous Improvement
news.summary_en: This brief summarizes the results of a network-based continuous improvement initiative to increase the proportion of students who are Black, Latino, or experiencing poverty and are on track for high school graduation and college enrollment.

news.title: Simpler Is Better for Autograders: Toward Cost-Effective LLM Evaluations for Open-Ended Tasks
news.summary_en: A simple rubric-based autograder outperformed more-complex large language model grading methods across benchmarks, often matching or beating nonexpert human graders while cutting down evaluation time and cost.
